description: Initialize cargo-dist in workspace — create dist-workspace.toml, add [profile.dist] to Cargo.toml, generate release.yml
steps: Install cargo-dist locally: cargo install cargo-dist --locked
Run `cargo dist init` in workspace root with options: ci=github, installers=[shell, powershell], targets=[x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu, x86_64-apple-darwin, aarch64-apple-darwin, x86_64-pc-windows-msvc]
Verify dist-workspace.toml is created with correct settings per spec §5.2
Verify [profile.dist] inherits = 'release' is added to Cargo.toml per spec §5.3
Verify .github/workflows/release.yml is generated (replaces hand-rolled version)

Configure additional settings in dist-workspace.toml: install-updater = true, github-attestations = true, dispatch-releases = true, unix-archive = '.tar.xz', windows-archive = '.zip', checksum = 'sha256', install-path = 'CARGO_HOME'
Run `cargo dist plan` locally to verify both aipm and aipm-pack are listed as apps and libaipm is excluded
Verify 4 target triples are listed in plan output

description: Remove hand-rolled release.yml and replace with cargo-dist generated workflow
steps: Back up current .github/workflows/release.yml for reference
Verify the cargo-dist generated release.yml triggers on tags matching v[0-9]+.[0-9]+.[0-9]+ (compatible with release-plz tags)
Verify the generated workflow includes workflow_dispatch trigger (dispatch-releases = true)
Verify the generated workflow has permissions: contents write, id-token write, attestations write
Verify the workflow has plan, build, host, and announce jobs
Verify the announce job publishes the release (not draft)
Confirm release-plz.yml and release-plz.toml remain unchanged

description: Shell installer script generation — aipm-installer.sh and aipm-pack-installer.sh uploaded as release assets
steps: Verify 'shell' is in the installers list in dist-workspace.toml
Run `cargo dist plan` and confirm shell installer is listed in the plan output
After first release: verify aipm-installer.sh is uploaded as a GitHub Release asset
After first release: verify aipm-pack-installer.sh is uploaded as a GitHub Release asset
Test: curl --proto '=https' --tlsv1.2 -LsSf .../aipm-installer.sh | sh installs aipm to ~/.cargo/bin
Test: installed binary runs correctly (aipm --version)
Test: installer adds binary to PATH correctly

description: PowerShell installer script generation — aipm-installer.ps1 and aipm-pack-installer.ps1 uploaded as release assets
steps: Verify 'powershell' is in the installers list in dist-workspace.toml
Run `cargo dist plan` and confirm PowerShell installer is listed in the plan output
After first release: verify aipm-installer.ps1 is uploaded as a GitHub Release asset
After first release: verify aipm-pack-installer.ps1 is uploaded as a GitHub Release asset
Test: irm .../aipm-installer.ps1 | iex installs aipm on Windows
Test: installed binary runs correctly (aipm --version)
Test: installer updates PATH via registry correctly

description: Self-updater (axoupdater) — updater binary included in release archives
steps: Verify install-updater = true is set in dist-workspace.toml
Run `cargo dist plan` and confirm updater is listed in the plan output
After first release: verify updater binary is included inside platform archives alongside main binary
Test: updater binary detects current version
Test: updater checks for newer releases on GitHub

description: Published GitHub Releases — releases are published (not draft) with all artifacts
steps: Verify the cargo-dist generated workflow creates published releases (not draft)
After first release: verify GitHub Release is visible on the public Releases page
Verify release contains 8 archives (2 binaries x 4 targets) per spec §5.8
Verify release contains 8 SHA256 checksum files
Verify release contains 4 installer scripts (shell + powershell for each binary)
Verify release contains source tarball
Test: gh attestation verify ./aipm --owner thelarkinn succeeds

description: cargo-binstall auto-discovery validation — verify cargo binstall aipm works without explicit metadata
steps: After first release: run cargo binstall aipm on a clean machine
Verify cargo-binstall auto-discovers the GitHub Release artifacts from cargo-dist
Verify correct platform binary is downloaded (not compiled from source)
If auto-discovery fails: add [package.metadata.binstall] to crates/aipm/Cargo.toml and crates/aipm-pack/Cargo.toml as fallback per spec §5.4
Test: cargo binstall aipm-pack also works

description: End-to-end release pipeline test — trigger a test release and verify full pipeline
steps: Ensure all cargo-dist config is committed and pushed to main
Either push a test tag (v0.1.1) or use workflow_dispatch to trigger the release
Verify release-plz.yml is NOT affected (still works for version bumps)
Verify cargo-dist release.yml triggers and all 4 platform builds succeed
Verify GitHub Release is published with all expected artifacts (spec §5.8)
Download and run each binary on its target platform
Test shell installer on macOS and Linux
Test PowerShell installer on Windows
Verify SHA256 checksums match: shasum -a 256 -c *.sha256
Verify attestation: gh attestation verify ./aipm --owner thelarkinn
